mnemonics: Pandas Serve Data: Series (1D) and DataFrames (2D) are the primary objects for holding and serving up your data, just like a panda serves up cuteness.
keyPoints: A Series is a one-dimensional array-like object holding data of any type, with an associated index.
A DataFrame is a two-dimensional, table-like structure with rows and columns, where each column is a Series.
Both Series and DataFrames have an index, which labels the data and enables powerful data alignment and selection.
commonMistakes: Treating a DataFrame like a list and using integer-based indexing (e.g., df[0]) to select a row, which actually selects a column. The correct method for row selection by integer is .iloc[0].
Forgetting that operations between Series objects align on their index labels, which can lead to unexpected NaN values if indexes don't match.
